methods.description: This method uses general linear models (GLM) to study the effects of temperature ('T') and relative humidity ('RH') on T. urticae and phytoseiid performance. It involves separate analyses for T. urticae and phytoseiids, focusing on survival, predation, and oviposition, with species-specific differences as a primary goal.

steps.description: Use a GLM with a multinomial distribution of the error and a generalized logit link function for survival analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform GLM with multinomial distribution for survival analysis

steps.description: Use a GLM with a Poisson distribution of the error and a logistic link function for predation and oviposition analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform GLM with Poisson distribution for predation and oviposition analysis
outputs: GLM predation and oviposition analysis results

steps.description: Include 'species', 'T', and 'RH' as fixed effects in all GLM analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: include fixed effects in GLM analyses

steps.description: Start analyses by considering all combinations, including 'species' as a factor, to identify species-specific differences.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze all combinations with species factor for species-specific differences

steps.description: Continue analyses of survival, predation, and oviposition by studying the effect of 'T', 'RH', and their interaction.
steps.searchable_summary: study effects of T, RH, and interaction on survival, predation, and oviposition

steps.description: Include 'replicate' as a random factor in the analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: include replicate as random factor in GLM analyses

steps.description: Use the Bonferroni post hoc test for mean separation at p < .05 when necessary.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Bonferroni post hoc test for mean separation

steps.description: Analyze all data using SPSS 23.0 software.
